newsSince: Material activity since June 9. On June 17 TTM announced all-cash acquisitions of Swiss Technology Group AG (Zurich) and ILFA GmbH (Hannover), its first European foothold, adding advanced/rigid-flex and miniaturized PCB capacity for medical, A&D and industrial; expected to close Q3 2026 and be modestly accretive. Also June 17 Mini-Xinger RF achieved AEC-Q200 automotive qualification, and June 3 TTM upsized financing with a new $1.0B revolver and a $400M Term Loan B add-on.

sources.label: TTM to acquire STG and ILFA (GlobeNewswire, Jun 17 2026)

verdicts.rankRationale: Genuinely new: June 17 dual European PCB acquisitions (STG/ILFA, accretive Q3 close) plus a fresh $1.0B revolver/$400M TLB and AEC-Q200 qualification expand capacity and end-market reach. Fundamentally positive, but on this crowding-sensitive page the +200% YTD rerating still dominates, so only a small +1 and hold rank 64.
verdicts.bull: Record AI server/DC&Networking demand (+61% YoY segment), now adding European advanced/rigid-flex capacity and balance-sheet firepower for further M&A.
verdicts.neutral: Strong franchise and accretive deals, but the stock has already rerated ~3x YTD; alpha now mostly priced in.
verdicts.bear: Crowded Strong-Buy consensus at $22B cap; integration and leverage risk; any AI capex pause hits the rerated multiple hard.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: STG/ILFA close + continued AI/DC PCB demand
